rotate Element

The <rotate> element groups rotation data that rotates the frame in local space via quaternions.

<rotate
   value = string
/>

Required Attributes

None.

Optional Attributes

value

If specified, the value attribute should contain four space-delimited floating-point values comprising the quaternion by which to rotate the frame. In most cases, value will be "0 0 0 -1"—no rotation. If there is animation associated to the frame, this attribute should be left undefined. See the examples below.

Element Information

Parent Elements matrix
Child Elements animate, keyTimes, values

Examples

A. Simple Rotation Without Animation

In this example, the frame is rotated 90 degrees around the x-axis. Since this is a simple rotation, the <rotate> element has no children.

<rotate value="0 0.7071 0 0.7071"/>
B. Rotation With Animation

This rotation involves an animation that starts at 0 seconds and lasts for 36 seconds. It is broken up into multiple key times (ranging from 0 to 1.) Each key time corresponds to a single rotation by a quaternion. For example, at key time 0.5, the frame will be rotated by the quaternion (0, -1, 0, 7.54979e-008). Because there is animation with multiple rotations, the value attribute of the <rotate> element is undefined.

<rotate>
   <animate begin="0s" dur="36s"/>
   <values>
      0 0 0 -1
      0 -1 0  7.54979e-008
      0 -1.50996e-007 0 -1
   </values>
   <keyTimes>
      0
      0.5
      1
   </keyTimes>
</rotate>
